title: A note on certain scenarios of Type II blowups of suitable weak solutions to the Navier-Stokes equations

thm:2.1.summary: Rules out Type II blowup scenario (1.2)+(1.4) when m₀ < f(m). Uses Euler scaling (2.7) and contradiction via Hölder inequality: the assumed blowup rate forces $\varepsilon$₀$\lambda^\gamma$ to diverge, which is impossible under the uniform bound M₁.

rem:2.2.summary: Notes that [Seregin2023] imposed the stricter identity (2.5) relating m and m₀. Theorem 2.1 shows this is unnecessary — the weaker inequality (2.3) suffices to exclude the blowup scenario.

thm:3.1.summary: Key reduction: if the modified blowup scenario (3.1)+(1.4)+(3.5) occurs, there exists a nontrivial ancient solution (u,p) to the Euler equations in Q₋ $= \mathbb{R}^3\times(-\infty,0)$ satisfying bound (3.7), energy inequality (3.9), and the nontriviality condition $M^{s,l}_{\kappa,m$₀$}(u,1) \ge \varepsilon$₀/2. Reduces blowup exclusion to Liouville theorems for ancient Euler solutions.

prop:4.1.summary: Liouville theorem for self-similar Euler profiles: U ≡ 0 for 1/2 < m < 3/5 (i.e., 0 < m₁ < 1/5) under the growth condition (4.4) with $\gamma < (ln(2+m$₁) - ln 2)/(m₁ ln 2). Rules out self-similar blowup in this parameter range. Proof in [Seregin2023].

prop:4.2.summary: Conservation law for axisymmetric ancient Euler solutions with zero swirl: the functional g(t) = (2/l₁$)\int(|\omega_\theta|/r)^{l$₁/2} dx is constant in time. If $\omega_\theta(\cdot,t$₀) = 0 at any time, then u ≡ 0 in Q₋. Proof in [Seregin2023-1].

thm:5.1.summary: Rules out modified blowup scenario (s=l=3, $\kappa=2)$ under additional Ladyzhenskaya-Prodi-Serrin type condition v $\in L_{s₂,l₂}(Q)$ with 3/s₂ $+ (\alpha+1)/l$₂ $= \alpha$. Full proof via Theorem 3.1's ancient Euler solution, then two cases: (i) l₂ $< \infty$ — scaling forces u = 0 in Q; (ii) l₂ $= \infty$ — elaborate energy identity + pressure splitting (p₁+p₂) shows $u(\cdot,-T$₀) = 0 for all T₀, contradicting nontriviality.

rem:5.2.summary: Explains the Ladyzhenskaya-Prodi-Serrin name: when m = 1 (Type I), the identity (5.3) reduces to the classical LPS condition 3/s₂ + 2/l₂ = 1. Theorem 5.1 thus generalizes the LPS regularity theory to Type II blowup scenarios.

edges.note: Observes that Theorem 2.1 relaxes the identity (2.5) from [Seregin2023] to the inequality (2.3)
Uses the Euler scaling (2.7) introduced in proof of Theorem 2.1; necessary condition (3.3) derives from inequality (2.3)
Function class (4.2) for self-similar profiles derives from the ancient solution bounds (3.7) produced by Theorem 3.1
Applies to ancient Euler solutions satisfying conditions (3.7)-(3.9) from Theorem 3.1; additional restrictions (4.7)-(4.9) motivated by the blowup scenario
Proof invokes Theorem 3.1 to extract a nontrivial ancient Euler solution from the blowup sequence, then shows it must be trivial under LPS
Repeats the Euler scaling (2.7) from proof of Theorem 2.1 for the special case s=l=3, $\kappa=2, \lambda=r_k$
Interprets Theorem 5.1 as a generalization of classical LPS regularity theory to Type II scenarios

layer_1_scenario_exclusion.description: Direct exclusion of Type II blowup via Euler scaling and Hölder inequality for m₀ < f(m)

layer_2_blowup_to_euler.description: Reduces remaining blowup scenarios to existence of nontrivial ancient Euler solutions via compactness

layer_3_liouville_tools.description: Liouville theorems ruling out ancient Euler solutions: self-similar (Prop 4.1) and axisymmetric conservation (Prop 4.2)

layer_4_lps_regularity.description: Full regularity under Ladyzhenskaya-Prodi-Serrin condition: ancient Euler solution forced trivial via energy identity and pressure splitting
